title: Infrastructure Resilience Mental Models
objective: Build an immediate working picture of infrastructure resilience.
beginnerExplanation: Infrastructure Resilience is easier to learn when you understand what job the field does before you try to memorise its details. This starter lesson gives you the shape of the territory, the recurring trade-offs, and the kinds of real systems the field touches.
deeperExplanation:
In Nexus, Infrastructure Resilience is positioned as a starter subject pack first and a richer long-term discipline second. That means the immediate goal is orientation: how the field frames problems, what kinds of evidence it uses, and why it matters beyond its own silo.

Once that shape is clear, deeper study becomes much less fragmented because you can connect the field to the surrounding subjects, roles, and topics rather than treating it as isolated content.
keyTakeaways: Infrastructure Resilience has a distinct way of seeing problems
The field is best learned through systems and trade-offs
Applications make the theory feel real
The academy is designed to let you branch deeper later
example: Use infrastructure resilience to compare one real system, one institutional example, and one frontier question.
commonMistakes: Trying to memorise details before understanding the shape of the field
Studying the field in isolation from adjacent domains
Assuming the most visible public examples represent the whole field
exercise: Write a short map of how infrastructure resilience connects to at least three other subjects or topics in Nexus.

mentalModel.title: Infrastructure Resilience as a system
mentalModel.summary: Start with the field as a system of inputs, trade-offs, and outputs before diving into specialist details.

howItWorks.title: Starter process
steps: Define what the field is trying to explain or build
Identify the main mechanisms and constraints
Branch into deeper study only after the map is clear

downsides: A starter map is not a substitute for mastery
Some contested details remain simplified at this stage
teaching.workedExample: A starter infrastructure resilience analysis compares a real case, the field-specific lens, and the implications for adjacent domains.
realWorldApplications: Real decisions shaped by infrastructure resilience
whoWorksWithThis: Adjacent practitioners who depend on the same models
